audit_metadata.scope: Comprehensive security audit - SAST, Dependency Scan, API Security, Infrastructure
audit_metadata.revision_notes: Corrected CRIT-001 false positive - .env files are properly gitignored and not exposed in version control

executive_summary.recommendation: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED - Critical vulnerabilities identified including weak authentication configurations and unsafe deserialization

critical_findings.title: Weak Default JWT Secret Key
critical_findings.description: Default JWT secret key 'sentinel-dev-secret-key-change-in-production-12345678901234567890' is hardcoded and predictable
evidence: jwt_secret_key: str = Field(default='sentinel-dev-secret-key-change-in-production', description='JWT secret key for token signing')
critical_findings.impact: Token forgery, unauthorized access escalation, session hijacking, complete authentication bypass

steps: Generate cryptographically secure random JWT secret (minimum 256 bits/32 bytes)
Use python: secrets.token_urlsafe(32) or openssl rand -base64 32
Store in secure secret management system, never in code or .env files
Implement key rotation mechanism (monthly recommended)
Invalidate all existing JWT tokens by rotating the secret
Add production environment validation to fail startup if default secret detected

critical_findings.title: Unsafe Pickle Deserialization
critical_findings.description: pickle.load() used without input validation, vulnerable to arbitrary code execution
critical_findings.impact: Remote code execution (RCE), complete system compromise, malware injection, data exfiltration
owasp_mapping: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

steps: Replace pickle with safer serialization formats (JSON, MessagePack, Protocol Buffers)
If pickle is required, implement signature validation using HMAC
Use restricted pickle unpickler with find_class() override to whitelist allowed classes
Validate file integrity before deserialization (checksum, digital signature)
Isolate pickle deserialization in sandboxed environment
Add Content Security Policy (CSP) to prevent malicious pickle injection

high_findings.title: Weak Default Admin Credentials
high_findings.description: Default admin account uses predictable credentials (admin@sentinel.com / admin123)
high_findings.impact: Administrative account compromise, privilege escalation, full system takeover

steps: Force password change on first login for default admin account
Implement strong password requirements (min 12 chars, complexity rules)
Add multi-factor authentication (MFA) for admin accounts
Disable default admin account in production environments
Create admin accounts through secure provisioning process
Implement account lockout after failed login attempts

high_findings.title: Hardcoded Database Credentials
high_findings.description: Database password 'sentinel_password' is hardcoded in multiple configuration files
high_findings.impact: Database compromise, data theft, data manipulation, unauthorized access to sensitive information

steps: Generate strong random database password (minimum 16 characters)
Store database credentials in Docker secrets or Kubernetes secrets
Use IAM-based authentication for cloud-hosted databases (AWS RDS IAM)
Implement least-privilege database user roles
Enable database connection encryption (SSL/TLS)
Rotate database credentials quarterly

high_findings.title: Overly Permissive CORS Headers
high_findings.description: CORS allows all headers with wildcard '*' configuration
evidence: cors_allow_headers: List[str] = Field(default=['*'], description='Allowed CORS headers')
high_findings.impact: Cross-origin attacks, header injection, credential theft, CSRF vulnerabilities

steps: Replace wildcard '*' with explicit header whitelist
Allow only required headers: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization', 'X-Correlation-ID']
Implement strict CORS origin validation (no wildcards)
Set Access-Control-Max-Age to limit preflight cache
Review and restrict allow_credentials to specific origins
Implement CSRF token validation for state-changing operations

high_findings.title: Docker Container Running as Root
high_findings.description: Production Docker containers run with root privileges, violating least-privilege principle
evidence: No USER directive found in Dockerfiles
high_findings.impact: Container escape vulnerabilities, host system compromise, privilege escalation

steps: Create non-root user in Dockerfile: RUN useradd -m -u 1000 sentinel
Switch to non-root user: USER sentinel
Set proper file permissions for application directories
Use read-only root filesystem: docker run --read-only
Drop unnecessary capabilities: --cap-drop=ALL
Implement security context in kubernetes: runAsNonRoot: true

high_findings.title: Default RabbitMQ Credentials
high_findings.description: RabbitMQ using default guest/guest credentials exposed on network
high_findings.impact: Message interception, unauthorized queue access, denial of service, message tampering

steps: Create unique RabbitMQ credentials (strong username/password)
Store credentials in Docker secrets
Enable RabbitMQ TLS/SSL encryption for connections
Implement RabbitMQ access control policies (vhost isolation)
Disable guest user or restrict to localhost only
Enable RabbitMQ audit logging

high_findings.title: Dynamic Import with __import__
high_findings.description: Dynamic module import without input validation can lead to code injection
high_findings.impact: Arbitrary code execution, module hijacking, malicious code injection

steps: Implement strict whitelist of allowed modules
Validate module names against regex pattern: ^[a-z0-9_]+$
Use importlib.import_module() with validation
Implement module signature verification
Restrict import paths to known safe directories
Add logging for all dynamic imports

high_findings.title: Exposed Observability Ports
high_findings.description: Prometheus (9090) and Jaeger (16686) exposed without authentication
high_findings.impact: Sensitive metrics exposure, system architecture disclosure, performance data leakage

steps: Implement authentication for Prometheus and Jaeger UIs
Use reverse proxy (nginx) with OAuth2/OIDC authentication
Restrict port access to internal networks only
Implement IP whitelist for monitoring endpoints
Enable TLS encryption for monitoring traffic
Consider using VPN or bastion host for access

medium_findings.title: In-Memory User Store
medium_findings.description: User credentials stored in memory dictionary, not persistent database
evidence: # In-memory user store (replace with database in production)
medium_findings.impact: Session loss on restart, data loss, scalability issues, no audit trail

steps: Migrate user storage to PostgreSQL database
Implement proper user model with SQLAlchemy ORM
Add database migrations using Alembic
Implement user audit logging (login, logout, password changes)
Add session persistence to database or Redis
Implement proper transaction management

medium_findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting Implementation
medium_findings.description: Rate limit configuration exists but no implementation in API endpoints
evidence: No rate limiting middleware found in services
medium_findings.impact: Denial of service attacks, API abuse, resource exhaustion, cost escalation

steps: Implement rate limiting using slowapi or fastapi-limiter
Add rate limiting middleware to all API services
Implement per-user and per-IP rate limits
Add rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining)
Implement distributed rate limiting using Redis for multi-instance deployments
Add rate limit monitoring and alerting

medium_findings.title: Outdated Frontend Dependencies
medium_findings.description: Several React and testing library dependencies are outdated and may contain known vulnerabilities
medium_findings.impact: Known vulnerabilities, security patches missing, compatibility issues

steps: Run npm audit to identify known vulnerabilities
Update all dependencies to latest stable versions
Review and test application after updates
Implement automated dependency scanning (Dependabot, Snyk)
Add npm audit to CI/CD pipeline
Set up automated security alerts for new vulnerabilities

medium_findings.title: Insufficient Security Event Logging
medium_findings.description: Missing security event logging for authentication failures, authorization denials, and suspicious activities
medium_findings.impact: Inability to detect attacks, no forensic evidence, compliance violations (SOC2, GDPR)
owasp_mapping: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

steps: Implement comprehensive security event logging
Log all authentication events (login, logout, password change)
Log authorization failures with user context
Implement log aggregation (ELK stack, Splunk)
Add security alerting for suspicious patterns
Ensure PII is not logged or is properly masked
Implement log retention policy (minimum 90 days for security events)

medium_findings.title: Missing Input Validation in Agent Payloads
medium_findings.description: Security agent test payloads may not be properly sanitized before execution
evidence: XSS payloads contain eval() and executable code
Command injection payloads may be executed without sanitization
medium_findings.impact: Potential code execution if payloads are mishandled, test environment compromise

steps: Implement strict input validation for all agent payloads
Sanitize and escape payloads before execution
Run security testing agents in isolated sandboxes
Implement payload validation against known attack patterns
Add rate limiting for security testing operations
Ensure test payloads are never executed in production

low_findings.title: Missing Security Headers
low_findings.description: HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) are not implemented
low_findings.impact: Increased risk of XSS, clickjacking, MITM attacks

steps: Add security headers middleware to all FastAPI services
Implement Content-Security-Policy with strict directives
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN
Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Add Referrer-Policy: no-referrer or strict-origin-when-cross-origin

low_findings.title: Verbose Error Messages
low_findings.description: Error messages may expose internal system details (stack traces, database info)
evidence: debug: bool = Field(default=False) # But may be enabled
Error responses may include sensitive details
low_findings.impact: Information disclosure, architecture reconnaissance, debugging assistance for attackers

steps: Implement custom error handler to sanitize error responses
Remove stack traces from production error responses
Log detailed errors server-side, return generic messages to clients
Ensure debug mode is disabled in production
Implement error codes instead of detailed messages
Add error monitoring to track and fix issues

low_findings.title: API Documentation Exposed Without Authentication
low_findings.description: FastAPI automatic documentation (/docs, /redoc) accessible without authentication
evidence: No authentication required for /docs or /redoc endpoints
low_findings.impact: API structure disclosure, endpoint enumeration, parameter discovery

steps: Disable automatic docs in production: docs_url=None, redoc_url=None
Implement authentication for documentation endpoints
Host documentation separately with access controls
Use custom documentation solution for production
Ensure OpenAPI spec doesn't expose sensitive endpoints
Implement role-based access to documentation

python_dependencies.notes: No requirements.txt found in sentinel_backend root. Dependencies managed via poetry (pyproject.toml and poetry.lock). Manual CVE check required using 'poetry audit' or 'safety check'.

rust_dependencies.known_issues: No immediate high-severity CVEs identified. Recommend running 'cargo audit' for comprehensive scan.

vulnerable_packages: postcss@8.4.16 - Known vulnerabilities in older versions
frontend_dependencies.recommendation: Run 'npm audit fix' and update to latest stable versions

API1_Broken_Object_Level_Authorization.notes: RBAC implemented with permission-based access control

API2_Broken_Authentication.notes: Weak JWT secret and default admin credentials

API7_Server_Side_Request_Forgery.notes: URL validation in spec_service needs review

API10_Unsafe_Consumption_of_APIs.notes: External API call validation needs review

immediate_actions: Generate and deploy new JWT secret key (CRIT-002)
Replace pickle deserialization with safe alternatives (CRIT-003)
Change all default credentials (HIGH-001, HIGH-002, HIGH-005)
short_term: Implement rate limiting across all services
Configure Docker containers to run as non-root
long_term: Implement secret management system (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
Add multi-factor authentication for admin accounts
Implement automated dependency scanning in CI/CD
Set up centralized logging and SIEM
Implement security training for development team

SOC2: No encryption at rest for sensitive data
Missing access reviews and audit trails

false_positives_corrected.original_title: Exposed API Keys in Environment Files
false_positives_corrected.correction_reason: .env files are properly gitignored (lines 105-109 in .gitignore) and not committed to version control
false_positives_corrected.verification: git check-ignore -v confirmed all .env files are ignored
false_positives_corrected.impact: This was a false positive. API keys in .env files are NOT exposed in version control and are properly protected.
